summary: PR 38 replaced an 0x84-byte incbin at lbl_803C1154 in asm/data.s with an explicit table of 33 fighter OnLoad function pointers, and made the referenced code locations global labels in asm/text_2.s. The change exposed the Fighter OnLoad table as relocatable assembly data instead of opaque binary data, enabling future naming and decompilation work to refer directly to per-character OnLoad routines. Evidence is limited to the title and diff; there were no PR body notes, reviews, or comments.
what_changed: In asm/data.s, lbl_803C1154 was changed from `.incbin "baserom.dol", 0x3BE154, 0x84` into an explicit `.4byte` table of function pointers.
The table at lbl_803C1154 contains 33 entries, matching the removed 0x84 bytes of data as 33 4-byte pointers.

The explicit table includes pointers such as func_800E0960, func_800E57AC, func_800E2AEC, func_8010D9AC, func_800EE680, func_80132ABC, func_800EAE44, func_801100EC, func_8011480C, FighterOnLoad_Peach, and many later func_8014xxxx/func_80155E1C entries.
Added `.balign 4` before lbl_803C1154 and before lbl_803C11D8 in asm/data.s, preserving table alignment around the newly disassembled data.
In asm/text_2.s, added `.global` labels at every function entry referenced by the OnLoad pointer table.
Added a named global symbol `FighterOnLoad_Peach` at address 8011B628, while most other entries remained address-derived `func_XXXXXXXX` names.
Added `stub_8014FC68` as a separate global symbol for a standalone `blr` immediately before func_8014FC6C, preventing that stub from being conflated with the following OnLoad routine.
smart_moves: Converted exactly 0x84 bytes of opaque data into 33 `.4byte` entries, preserving the original size while making the table searchable and linkable.
Declared every referenced target as `.global` before using it in the data table, avoiding unresolved-symbol problems and making cross-file references explicit.
Kept unknown functions under address-based `func_XXXXXXXX` names instead of inventing character or behavior names without evidence.
Used the known semantic name `FighterOnLoad_Peach` where apparently available, demonstrating incremental naming rather than all-or-nothing table naming.
Separated a one-instruction `blr` stub as `stub_8014FC68` before labeling func_8014FC6C, which is a useful tactic when table targets are adjacent to padding or stub code.
decomp_lessons: When an incbin region is known to be a pointer table, replace it with `.4byte` symbol references of the same total byte size to improve relocation visibility without changing layout.
For early decomp work, exposing data tables can be valuable even before all pointed-to functions are fully named or translated to C.
Address-derived labels are acceptable placeholders when semantics are not yet proven; only apply descriptive names when evidence exists.
Adding globals at function boundaries enables data sections to reference text symbols directly and helps future tooling find call/data references.
Explicit alignment directives around disassembled data help preserve section layout when replacing incbins with assembler directives.
naming_conventions: Unknown function entry points were named with the project’s address-based form `func_XXXXXXXX`, for example `func_800E0960` and `func_80155E1C`.
A known fighter-specific OnLoad function used the descriptive form `FighterOnLoad_Peach`.
A trivial return-only routine was labeled with the `stub_XXXXXXXX` convention: `stub_8014FC68`.
Unidentified data labels retained generic address-based labels such as `lbl_803C1154` and `lbl_803C11D8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced `.incbin` data with `.4byte` symbol references while preserving the exact 0x84-byte footprint.
Inserted `.balign 4` around the pointer table to maintain 4-byte alignment for function pointers and following data.
Added `.global` directives at text labels so that asm/data.s could legally reference those text symbols.
Maintained unknown routines in assembly and only added labels; this was a disassembly/symbolization step rather than a C matching conversion.
Isolated `stub_8014FC68` as its own symbol before `func_8014FC6C`, a boundary-marking tactic useful near adjacent `blr` instructions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename the remaining `func_XXXXXXXX` Fighter OnLoad entries to character-specific names.
Search for later changes to `lbl_803C1154` to see whether the table receives a semantic label or C declaration.
Search for uses of `FighterOnLoad_Peach` to identify the naming pattern eventually used for other fighters.
Check whether `stub_8014FC68` remains a standalone stub or is later explained as padding, an empty OnLoad, or a table-adjacent boundary artifact.
